methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Quantum Machines is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $100M+ to date. The broader quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies—3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private entities including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and Rigetti Computing—providing directional market context though limited narrow peer precedent at identical stage and valuation tier.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation approach given insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage peers within quantum computing (fewer than three datapoints in narrow peer filters). Using $100M in total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement yields an implied valuation of $190M. This single-method result constrains confidence; peer-median analysis was not applicable due to sparse high-confidence comparables at similar scale and stage.

The supported valuation range is $162M to $218M, centered at $190M. Key uncertainties include the absence of robust same-stage peer multiples to validate the 1.9x progression assumption and the nascent, heterogeneous nature of quantum computing business models, which limits cross-company revenue and profitability comparisons. Any valuation near these levels should be stress-tested against the company's specific technical differentiation, capital efficiency relative to peers, and time-to-revenue assumptions.
